title: spergula rubra subsp. radicans
intro: Spergula rubra subsp. radicans, also known as creeping or running sandwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. Spergula rubra subsp. radicans can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety are still being researched and documented.
